title: Built a trading agent after seeing what an llm could do without tools. Thoughts?
author: getstonkzilla
selftext:
LuckyOne7777 ([https://github.com/LuckyOne7777](https://github.com/LuckyOne7777)) ran an experiment where ChatGPT managed a $100 micro-cap trading portfolio completely on its own. I found this quite interesting and watched it over the end of 2025.

The results: Down 20% while the s&p was up 10%. A stock (atyr) tanked 75% overnight. The model kept re-entering losing positions, got attached to narratives, lost strategies, etc. This was before the likes of openclaw and hermes agents.

However, remove atyr and the profit factor was 1.52. Lucky managed to create something that could generate some sort of profit.

Lucky’s gpt was given raw price data and a few metrics, then asked to make trading decisions through conversation. No tools, pipeline or guardrail.

But what if you gave an agent tools?  I use hermes (using mimo-V2-pro) on a dedicated server, with tools and a lot of data.

The idea: don't have the llm do everything. Give it focused tools that return small sets of data, then let it make decisions based on rules. The agent chains them together to execute trades.

How the pipe works: I pay for a data api. It provides quite a lot; but quite a lot needs to be manipulated in order to produce something usable. This is all done on vps and complete by 2pm MYT each trading day.

Then my agent runs and has access to the following tools:

\- composite\_value\_scan.js - ranked scan combining f-Score + ev/ebitda + magic formula  
\- confluence\_scan.js - technical confluence (rsi, bb, macd) with f-Score scoring  
\- fscore\_scan.js - piotroski f-Score screen (0-9), pure quality filter  
\- ev\_ebitda\_scan.js - cheapest stocks by ev/ebitda (acquirer's multiple)  
\- magic\_formula\_scan.js - greenblatt's earnings yield + return on capital ranking  
\- momentum\_scan.js - rsi, macd, volume momentum plays  
\- bb\_bounce\_scan.js - oversold at lower bollinger band, mean reversion setups  
\- bb\_breakout\_scan.js - bb squeeze breakout, consolidation breakouts  
\- sector\_rs\_scan.js - sector relative strength, leading vs lagging sectors  
\- sentiment\_surge\_scan.js - social momentum plays  
\- sentiment\_contrarian\_scan.js - fear-based contrarian setups  
\- swing\_trade\_setup.js - f-Score stocks at support with r:r ratios and atr stops

\- stock\_detail.js - full stock profile: price, technicals, fundamentals, sentiment, last 30 news articles, 30/90/365d performance, atr(14)  
\- support\_resistance.js - s/r levels from swing highs/lows + bb+ smas, entry/exit planning  
\- market\_context.js - f&greed index, regime, trend direction, spy performance

\- portfolio\_summary.js - cash, positions, total p&l, win rate, drawdown  
\- positions\_monitor.js - open positions with live p&l and s1/target alerts  
\- position\_health\_check.js - exit signals: r1 touch, atr support break, f-Score drop, capital stuck  
\- trade\_history.js - past trades with stats  
\- watchlist.js - stocks waiting for support levels

\- execute\_trade.js - buy/sell/scale\_in/scale\_out.  
\- scale\_in\_scan.js - identify winners worth adding to  
\- scale\_out\_scan.js - partial profit-taking opportunities

If it is happy, it will buy at the closing price. Max 1 buy per day, max 25% of portfolio in any position. If nothing qualifies, do nothing.

The exit strategy - atr-based buffers - the stop sits below support, sized by the stock's own volatility. Calm stocks get tight stops, wild stocks get more room. The price has to close below the stop, not just wick through it. This filters out stop hunts.

Take profit targets are stored at entry - when the agent buys, it records the R1 resistance level as its exit target. During daily checks, if the intraday high touched that target, it fills at the target price. This mimics a limit order works - when touched. Support breaks are different - those need close confirmation, so those fill at the close.

Execution timing - this is paper trading. All buys happen at the closing price after the market shuts down, no live intraday execution.

I have ohlc data. The system uses high and low for detection: did price touch resistance? Did it wick below support? But the actual buy fill is always the Close. Take profit sells fill at the target (limit order logic). Support break sells fill at the close (need confirmation).

Pull this strat apart, maybe you think it is shit; I dunno. Ill share everything here as the agent trades.

comments.author: getstonkzilla
comments.body:
TARS Daily | Apr 30, 2026

$862.52 cash | \~$1,000.00 portfolio

POSITIONS:

\- ALLE — $137.48 (+0.0%) 0d | S1 $136.46 | ATR Stop $130.24 | Target $143.30

TRADES:

\- BUY ALLE 1 share @ $137.48 | take\_profit $143.30 — BB Lower support, F8, 60% composite, R1 +4.2%, R:R 6.0:1. Earnings miss priced in, next Jul 23.

MARKET: F&G 67 (Greed, Rising) — +52 from month ago (was 15 extreme fear). Stable regime. Selective mode: only best setups.

TARS THINKING: Fresh portfolio, first trade. 25 setups in the scan but most are noise at this F&G level.

Top 3: BCPC (F8, SMA200), DPZ (F9, BB Lower), ALLE (F8, BB Lower). All passed real S/R verification (>3% to R1, >1.5:1 R:R).

BCPC got axed — earnings were Apr 30, stock dropped -6.76% on results. That is a binary event, full stop. F8 means nothing when the market is actively repricing the company post-earnings. Wait for it to stabilize.

DPZ is a falling knife hiding behind a good F-Score. Down -30% over the year, -17% in 90 days, composite fundamentals 47% with Health at 1/6. The F-Score measures accounting quality — DPZ has clean books but the market is telling you something is structurally wrong. Price sits well below SMA200 ($412 vs $339), making SMA200 resistance, not support. I am not catching this one.

ALLE made the cut. F8, fair 60% composite, BB Lower support at $136.46. The Q1 earnings miss (EPS $1.80 vs est.) is priced in — stock barely moved at close (+0.08%). Revenue was up 9.7%. DCI acquisition integration and ERP issues are real but not existential. Next earnings Jul 23 gives us nearly 3 months of runway. R1 is SMA20 at $143.30 (+4.2%), and the ATR stop at $130.24 gives a wide 5.3% buffer against stop hunting.

Position sizing: 1 share at $137.48 = 13.7% of portfolio. Conservative start given Greed regime and the recent earnings overhang. Room to add if it holds support.

The bigger story: F&G has gone from 15 (extreme fear) to 67 (greed) in one month. That is a massive swing. When sentiment reverses this fast, the easy value buys dry up. The scan found 25 setups but most had weak fundamentals or thin R:R after real resistance checks. Discipline matters more than action in this regime.

SCAN: 25 found -> 3 passed S/R verification -> 2 passed news -> 1 executed

comments.author: Opening-Berry-6041
comments.body: dude that initial gpt agent with no tools going down 20% is wild like how did it even get so attached to bad trades without any real data or logic programmed in?

replies.author: getstonkzilla
replies.body: atyr crashed and the portfolio lost 40% in one day. Also its instructions were to stay under 300m caps, more volatility
